title: centaurea paniculata subsp. castellana
intro: Centaurea paniculata subsp. castellana is a subspecies of perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly known as the knapweed. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. Centaurea paniculata subsp. castellana can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to leverage its medicinal properties.
